name: Beef Stir-Fry with Vegetables
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Good-quality sirloin tips, cut into 1-inch cubes
2 tbsp Vegetable oil (1 tbsp plus 1/2 teaspoon)
2 Large stalks of broccoli, stems removed and cut into florets
2 Carrots, peeled and sliced on a diagonal
1 Scallion, both white and green parts chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Marinate the sirloin at room temperature in a mixture of sherry, soy sauce, 1 tablespoon of cornstarch, sugar, 1/2 tablespoon of vegetable oil, pepper, and garlic for an hour.
2. Heat 1 tablespoon of the remaining vegetable oil in a wok over high heat.
3. Stir-fry beef quickly until the meat is brown outside and pink inside. Remove from the wok.
4. Mix the remaining 2 teaspoons cornstarch with the oyster sauce.
5. Reheat the wok with the remaining tablespoon of oil. Add the broccoli and carrots; stir-fry for 30 seconds.
6. Add the broth, cover the wok, and steam for approximately 1 minute or until the vegetables are tender but retain their crunch.
7. Add the corn, snow peas, scallion, beef, and oyster sauce-cornstarch mixture. Heat quickly until the sauce is clear and thickened.
8. Serve immediately over rice or Chinese noodles. Makes 4 servings.
